findings.finding: workspace-sync.md grew from 699L (2026-05-07) → 783L (today) = +84L (+12.0%) — exceeds the 10% bloat threshold. Cumulative growth from the 632L baseline is +151L (+23.9%). Largest sections are Step 2 ('Sync workspace files to clone', 320L) and Step 3.6 ('PROP-014 verify-pending-state + narrative-cite audit + deploy-watch', 173L).
findings.evidence: wc -l monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md = 783; awk-section breakdown: Step 1 34L, Step 2 320L, Step 3 36L, Step 3.5 98L, Step 3.6 173L, Step 4 21L, Step 4b 74L, Step 5 4L, Rules 10L, Cleanup 8L. Most recent additions are PROP-014 (verify-pending-state) and PROP-016 (NEVER_PUSH strand rescue) related blocks.
findings.recommendation: workspace-sync runs hourly on Haiku (cheap), so the cost impact is bounded. But the file is now ≥10% over threshold for the second consecutive measurement — worth tracking. Step 2 (320L) is the natural extraction candidate: it is largely shell-block boilerplate (smart_copy, NEVER_PUSH self-tests, sync loop) that could move to monitor/scripts/workspace-sync-step2.sh and be sourced. Defer the actual PROP until decider's churn settles (multiple PROP-014 amendments still in flight); a refactor-to-script needs a stable spec.

findings.finding: Decider total context (excl CLAUDE.md) grew from 2087L (2026-05-07 baseline) → 2191L = +104L (+5.0%) over 2 days. Two individual reference files crossed the 10% per-file threshold: decider-curmudgeon.md +40L (+15.0%) and decider-patches-and-selfapply.md +66L (+12.6%). Section '6b. Self-Apply Easy Patches' is now 538L of a single 590L file.
findings.evidence: Per-file deltas computed against prior tinker report's recorded baselines. decider-patches-and-selfapply.md grew from 524L → 590L; decider-curmudgeon.md from 266L → 306L. Recent grep of section 6b shows added content for PROP-014 Mech 1a WRITE-VERIFY discipline (line 452) and the revision-routed closure edge case (line 454) — substantive feature documentation, not boilerplate.
findings.recommendation: Total decider growth is still under the 10% agent-total threshold (5.0%), so this is not a fresh alarm — but it is the same trend flagged in 2026-05-07's report (then at +14.1% over the older baseline; relative re-baselining this run makes the percent look smaller, but absolute growth continues). Section 6b is one cohesive procedural block (clone → run patches → push → verify-closure) and resists clean sub-extraction without breaking the workflow. Hold off on a heavy refactor PROP. Recommend revisiting extraction once the PROP-014 amendment stack stabilizes.

findings.finding: PROP-009r2's verification_pattern (status=applied) is partially stale. The multi-grep test includes `grep -Fq 'pq.history.length>200' monitor/prompts/decider.md`, but pq.history was deprecated — decider.md line 277 explicitly notes 'pq.history field no longer exists in the live file. Append'. The remaining 5 grep checks all pass (PROP-009 precondition in decider.md and curmudgeon.md, prop-009-enforce.flag in decider.md/build.js/workspace-sync.md). The one stale grep makes the verification return STILL_BROKEN for an applied PROP.
findings.evidence: Per-grep breakdown: 5/6 PRESENT, only 'pq.history.length>200' MISSING. decider.md:277 reads '(append-only JSONL). pq.history field no longer exists in the live file. Append'.
findings.recommendation: Update PROP-009r2.verification_pattern to drop the stale `pq.history.length>200` grep, or replace it with a check for the new append-only JSONL pattern. Conservative path: file as a finding so operator can decide whether to edit a PROP that's already in 'applied' status. Not auto-fixing this run — touching applied-state PROP files needs human signoff per project convention.

findings.finding: PROP-015's verification_pattern has an awk syntax error: `awk '{exit ( <= 660 &&  >= 640) ? 0 : 1}'` is missing `$1` and produces 'syntax error' on every run. Additionally, the expected line range (640-660) no longer matches the current workspace-sync.md size (783L) — the file grew further after PROP-015 was applied.
findings.evidence: Direct exec of PROP-015 verification_pattern produces 'awk: cmd. line:1: syntax error'. wc -l monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md = 783, outside the 640-660 expected band.
findings.recommendation: Two-part fix: (a) add `$1` after the awk `(`; (b) revise the expected line range or drop the line-count gate entirely (the grep-for-comments pattern is the substantive check; line-count was a sanity rail that's now stale). Same conservative posture as T-2026-05-09-04: not auto-fixing because PROP-015 is in 'self-applied' status.

findings.finding: PROP-020 (drift-audit-split) and PROP-021 (curmudgeon-dispatcher-optimization) have vacuous self-reference verification_patterns. Both test essentially `test -f <PROP file> && grep -q '"verification_pattern"' <PROP file>` — i.e., 'does this PROP file exist and contain the literal string verification_pattern?'. That is true for any tinker-authored PROP, regardless of whether the proposal was implemented. Both PROPs return FIXED today, but the FIXED is meaningless.
findings.evidence: PROP-020.verification_pattern = `test -f monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-020-drift-audit-split.json && grep -q '"verification_pattern"' monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-020-drift-audit-split.json && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN`. PROP-021 follows the same pattern. Both proposals are still status='proposed' and no integration work has landed in build-scripts/ or curmudgeon prompts.
findings.recommendation: Author/operator: replace these self-reference patterns with real checks of the target file/script. PROP-020 should test for the precomputed-candidates artifact path; PROP-021 should test for dispatcher-routing strings in curmudgeon.md. Tinker should NOT mark these PROPs implemented based on the vacuous current pattern.

findings.finding: EXP-294 has been status='complete', integrated=false for ~46h. expansion-tracker.json shows it created 2026-05-07T13:21:25Z, status complete, never integrated. Last decider run was 78m ago and did not pick it up.
findings.evidence: Expansion-tracker filter: items where status==='complete' && !integrated && !routed_to_curmudgeon → 1 item: EXP-294.
findings.recommendation: Read EXP-294's expansion file to determine why decider has not integrated it (could be: routed to curmudgeon and tracker.routed_to_curmudgeon is unset, awaiting a precondition, or simply orphaned). If neither, this is a candidate handoff bug worth a follow-up tinker investigation.
